When To Use: Use for all social triage runs.
Default Handling: Apply only authorized social platform rules. Do not infer from public pages or unauthenticated views.
Examples / Notes: The agent must stop and report missing account access.

When To Use: Use whenever a social item needs executive awareness, decision, response, approval, or a new rule.
Default Handling: Create or update an item in the existing Executive Decision Inbox. Do not create a separate social inbox. The title must start with the exact source, such as Facebook Messenger:, LinkedIn Message:, LinkedIn Connection Request:, Facebook Group:, or Facebook Friend Request:.
Examples / Notes: Fill exact ask, suggested action, summary, why it matters, source thread link, source channel, and assistant notes. The page body must not be blank.

When To Use: Use whenever creating or updating an Executive Decision Inbox item from a social source.
Default Handling: Create a readable page body with Decision Brief, Exact Ask, Suggested Action, Context, Why It Matters, Source And Meta, and Run Notes. The Decision Brief must include sender, observed sent time, source channel, and the actual message or post text.
Examples / Notes: For DMs, include the full relevant inbound message transcript with timestamps and sender names. For group posts/comments, include the full relevant post/comment text. Fetch the Notion page after creation and verify it is not blank.

When To Use: Use whenever the executive comments in Notion, changes a decision item status, or marks an item handled.
Default Handling: Route the follow-up by Source Channel and Source Thread Link. Messenger items go back to Messenger, LinkedIn items go back to LinkedIn, Facebook Group items go back to the linked group item, and other sources go back to their linked source.
Examples / Notes: Do not assume follow-up actions belong in email. If the source link or authentication is missing, keep the item active and add a blocker note.

When To Use: Legal threats, regulatory escalation, extremely angry customers, public reputation risk, or personal reach-outs the executive would likely want to know about immediately.
Default Handling: Create or update the Notion decision item first, set priority to Urgent, then send a compact urgent notification with the Notion page URL.
Examples / Notes: Notification format: triage ID, priority, one-line brief, Notion URL.

When To Use: Inbound connection requests from credible people in the target market, industry, partner ecosystem, or customer segment.
Default Handling: Accept credible requests. Reject suspicious, low-trust, fake, or unrelated requests. When unsure, place in review.
Examples / Notes: Use profile quality, mutual connections, role relevance, and message context.

When To Use: Inbound friend requests from credible industry contacts, customers, community members, or known people.
Default Handling: Accept credible requests. Reject suspicious, fake, flirt-bait, or unrelated requests. When unsure, reject or review based on the user's risk preference.
Examples / Notes: Do not accept low-trust profiles just because they have mutual friends.

When To Use: Generic pitches, scammy openers, flirt bait, fake profiles, or suspicious low-context messages.
Default Handling: Ignore, delete, or ban according to platform and user permissions. Do not create executive-facing items.
Examples / Notes: If there is any legal, customer, or personal relationship risk, use a higher-signal rule.

When To Use: JV, affiliate, podcast, speaking, launch, promotion, sponsor, or strategic collaboration requests.
Default Handling: Route routine partnership requests to the partnership lead. Create a decision item only when the opportunity is high-value, personal, urgent, or requires executive judgment.
Examples / Notes: Archive generic vendor pitches that pretend to be partnerships.

When To Use: Affiliate commissions, payouts, tracking, payment status, commission disputes, refund disputes, or accounting questions.
Default Handling: Route to the finance lead with sender, platform, source link, exact ask, and any screenshots or message context.
Examples / Notes: If the person is extremely angry or threatening legal action, use Social 911 Urgent Alert.

When To Use: Product access, billing support, login trouble, account issues, bug reports, or customer help requests.
Default Handling: Try to point them to support or route to the support lead. Escalate angry or high-risk issues.
Examples / Notes: Do not make support items executive-facing unless they are urgent, personal, or reputation-sensitive.

When To Use: Requests for hands-on setup, implementation, consulting, done-for-you work, or agency services.
Default Handling: Acknowledge the request and route to the operations lead with name, platform, profile link, and exact ask.
Examples / Notes: Create an executive decision item only if the request is strategic or personal.

When To Use: A real friend, colleague, investor, partner, client, or known person is trying to reach the executive.
Default Handling: Qualify what they need. Create a decision inbox item when the relationship or ask appears meaningful.
Examples / Notes: If the agent believes the executive would probably want to know immediately, use Social 911 Urgent Alert.

When To Use: Pending group or community member requests.
Default Handling: Approve legitimate profiles that match the group. Reject suspicious, fake, spammy, or low-trust profiles.
Examples / Notes: If group questions reveal a support or sales issue, route accordingly.

When To Use: Pending group posts, comments, peer help, normal discussion, and respectful questions.
Default Handling: Approve positive posts, peer help, normal discussion, and respectful questions. Leave negative or support-heavy items for review.
Examples / Notes: Do not approve flames, harassment, or complaint threads without review.

When To Use: Negative feedback, concerns, support issues, complaint posts, or repeated community frustration.
Default Handling: Route to community/support with what happened, who said it, when, source link, and suggested response. Escalate to executive if public reputation risk is material.
Examples / Notes: If the customer is extremely angry, use Social 911 Urgent Alert.

When To Use: Harassment, threats, hate, spam attacks, abuse, or obvious bad-faith disruption.
Default Handling: Delete and ban only when the rule match is unambiguous and permissions allow it. Otherwise route to review.
Examples / Notes: Record the reason for any ban.

When To Use: The item may matter but does not clearly match an active rule.
Default Handling: Create a decision item asking how this should be handled in the future. Do not silently create new policy.
Examples / Notes: Use sparingly. Do not use for obvious spam.
